warnings: The Goldstone bosons (3 real scalars) are absorbed as longitudinal components of the massive gauge bosons; their dof are included in the gauge sector count (9 dof for 3 massive vectors).
The quartic coupling lambda is not a free parameter; it is radiatively generated via the Coleman-Weinberg mechanism and is fixed by beta_lambda. It is never scanned or used as a viability filter.
No fermions are included, so the model cannot explain baryogenesis or provide DM candidates at tree level. It is minimal for FOPT+GWS only.
approximations: High-temperature expansion of the one-loop finite-temperature effective potential is used, valid when the barrier is generated by thermal corrections (small field / T).
Gaussian approximation for the false-vacuum fraction is used for percolation (arXiv:2602.02829).
The Coleman-Weinberg mechanism at one loop is assumed to generate the scale; higher-order corrections are neglected.
The radial mode mass-squared m_chi^2 = beta_lambda * chi0^2 is computed at one loop.

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Complex scalar SU(2) doublet, Phi = (phi^+, phi^0)^T. After SSB, one real scalar (radial mode / pseudo-dilaton chi) remains as a physical particle; the 3 Goldstone components are absorbed as the longitudinal modes of the massive gauge bosons.

particle_content.name: W^a_mu
particle_content.notes: 3 SU(2) gauge bosons, each with 3 dof in the broken phase (massive vector). The 3 dof account for 2 transverse polarizations + 1 longitudinal polarization (absorbed Goldstone).

lagrangian_terms.name: gauge_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Pure SU(2) Yang-Mills kinetic term. W^a_{munu} = partial_mu W^a_nu - partial_nu W^a_mu + g * epsilon^{abc} W^b_mu W^c_nu.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Gauge-invariant scalar kinetic term. D_mu = partial_mu + i * g * W^a_mu * tau^a where tau^a = sigma^a/2 are the SU(2) generators.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Tree-level scalar potential. Classically scale-invariant: no quadratic mass term. The quartic coupling lambda is radiatively generated and fixed by the Coleman-Weinberg mechanism. Not a free parameter. Not needed by the backend.

free_parameters.name: g
free_parameters.notes: SU(2)_X gauge coupling. Must be in the perturbative regime (g < 4*pi). Determines gauge boson mass-over-vev ratio gb = g/2.

free_parameters.name: chi0
free_parameters.notes: Vacuum expectation value of the scalar field at zero temperature. Sets the scale of symmetry breaking and all particle masses. The PTA frequency band maps chi0 approximately in the 0.1-10000 GeV range for perturbative couplings.

beta_lambda.description: Coefficient of the one-loop beta function for the quartic coupling, also proportional to the radial mode mass squared. Fixed by gauge boson contributions.
beta_lambda.derivation: CW relation: beta_lambda = (1/(8*pi^2)) * (sum_B n_B * gb^4 - sum_F n_F * gf^4). Here only the 3 SU(2) gauge bosons contribute, each with gb = g/2 and n_B = 3, giving beta_lambda = (1/(8*pi^2)) * 3 * (g/2)^4 = 3*g^4/(128*pi^2).

m_chi2.description: Mass squared of the radial (pseudo-dilaton) mode at zero temperature

gb_scalar.description: Mass-over-vev ratio for the radial scalar (used as input to the backend)
gb_scalar.notes: This is the gb value for the scalar's contribution to the thermal mass. It is auto-computed by the backend from beta_lambda.

delta_V.description: Vacuum energy density difference between symmetric and broken phases at zero temperature

ssb_pattern.order_parameter: chi (radial mode of the complex scalar doublet Phi)
ssb_pattern.notes: The SU(2)_X gauge group is completely broken by the VEV of the scalar doublet. All three gauge bosons become massive with equal mass m_W = g * chi0 / 2.

vev_convention.notes: The scalar field is expanded around the background as Phi = (G1 + i*G2, (chi + h + i*G3)) / sqrt(2), where G_{1,2,3} are the Goldstone bosons (eaten by the massive gauge bosons), h is the physical radial mode, and chi is the classical background field used in the effective potential.

tree_level_potential.notes: Tree-level potential in terms of the background field chi. No quadratic mass term due to classical scale invariance. The quartic lambda is radiatively generated via CW; the backend does not require its value directly.

field_dependent_masses.notes: Three SU(2) gauge bosons each acquire field-dependent mass m_W^2 = g^2 * chi^2 / 4. Total 3 x 3 = 9 dof (massive vectors).

The physical radial mode (pseudo-dilaton) acquires mass radiatively via the CW mechanism. beta_lambda = 3*g^4/(128*pi^2). The scalar gb is automatically computed by the backend. This particle is the remnant of the 4 real scalar dof after 3 Goldstones are eaten.

thermal_masses.particle: W^a longitudinal (Debye mass for daisy resummation)
thermal_masses.notes: Longitudinal (electric) Debye mass for SU(2) gauge bosons. Standard result for SU(N) with scalar matter: m_D^2 = (2/3 + N_s/6) * g^2 * T^2, where N_s=2 (one complex doublet = 2 real scalars in fundamental). Gives m_D^2 = g^2 * T^2. The transverse (magnetic) modes get no Debye mass at leading order.

Thermal mass for the scalar radial mode from gauge boson loops at high temperature. The contribution from the gauge sector to the scalar self-energy gives m_D^2 = (3/16) * g^2 * T^2. Scalar self-interaction contributions (from lambda) are subleading due to the CW suppression.

The Goldstone bosons (eaten by the gauge bosons) have the same thermal mass as the radial scalar from gauge interactions. At high T in the symmetric phase, the Goldstones contribute to the scalar thermal mass. In the broken phase they are absorbed into the longitudinal gauge boson modes, which already have their own Debye mass. The net contribution to the effective potential's thermal mass coefficient is already captured by the backend's g2sq integral.

expression_symbols.description: Background value of the scalar radial field
One-loop beta function coefficient for the quartic coupling
Mass squared of the radial scalar mode
Mass-over-vev ratio for the scalar, equal to sqrt(beta_lambda)
Vacuum energy difference at zero temperature

assumptions: The model is classically scale-invariant at tree level: no explicit mass scales are introduced in the Lagrangian.
Radiative symmetry breaking via the Coleman-Weinberg mechanism: the SU(2)_X gauge boson loops generate a nontrivial minimum for the scalar potential.
beta_lambda > 0 is satisfied because only bosons (gauge bosons) contribute to the CW potential and no fermions are present.
Perturbative regime holds for the gauge coupling (g < 4*pi) to maintain one-loop CW validity.
No U(1)_Y factor: the model is pure SU(2)_X without hypercharge, so no Z-boson-like mixing or photon appears.
No fermions are added: the model is minimal for FOPT+GW production and does not address DM or baryogenesis.

The high-temperature expansion of the one-loop finite-T effective potential is valid for the entire parameter space of interest (small field / T expansion, valid when the barrier is thermally generated).
The Gaussian approximation for the false-vacuum fraction is used as implemented in the backend.
The radial mode mass and VEV are related by m_chi^2 = beta_lambda * chi0^2 at one-loop order.

backend_compatibility.notes: Single-field conformal model with all masses proportional to one scalar vev and no tree-level mass term. Exactly matches the backend requirements. The backend auto-computes the scalar gb from beta_lambda. The example usage in the backend confirms the pattern for a U(1) model; this SU(2) model extends it by replacing the single gauge boson (3 dof) with three gauge bosons (9 dof).
